fields.name: North-West University
fields.description: North-West University (NWU) is a comprehensive South African university with three campuses in Potchefstroom, Mahikeng, and Vanderbijlpark.

fields.name: Rhodes University
fields.description: Rhodes University is a prestigious research university in Makhanda (Grahamstown), Eastern Cape, known for its small-campus academic excellence.

fields.name: University of the Western Cape
fields.description: The University of the Western Cape (UWC) is a public university in Bellville, Cape Town, known for its commitment to social justice and academic excellence.

fields.name: University of the Free State
fields.description: The University of the Free State (UFS) is a multi-campus South African university headquartered in Bloemfontein with campuses in Qwaqwa and at the South Campus.

fields.name: Durban University of Technology
fields.description: Durban University of Technology (DUT) is a university of technology in KwaZulu-Natal with campuses in Durban and Pietermaritzburg (Midlands), offering career-focused Diplomas, Advanced Diplomas, and Bachelor degrees.

fields.name: Mangosuthu University of Technology
fields.description: Mangosuthu University of Technology (MUT) is a university of technology in Umlazi, Durban, offering Diplomas, Advanced Diplomas, and Bachelor degrees across Engineering, Applied & Health Sciences, and Management Sciences.

fields.name: Vaal University of Technology
fields.description: Vaal University of Technology (VUT) is a university of technology in Vanderbijlpark, Gauteng, offering Diplomas, Advanced Diplomas, and Bachelor degrees across Engineering, Applied & Computer Sciences, Human Sciences, and Management Sciences.

fields.name: Walter Sisulu University
fields.description: Walter Sisulu University (WSU) is a comprehensive university in the Eastern Cape with campuses in Mthatha, East London, Butterworth, and Komani, offering programmes across Engineering, Health Sciences, Natural Sciences, Humanities, Education, and Management Sciences.

fields.name: University of Fort Hare
fields.description: The University of Fort Hare (UFH) is a historically significant South African university with campuses in Alice, East London, and Bhisho in the Eastern Cape. It offers programmes across Education, Law, Management & Commerce, Science & Agriculture, Social Sciences & Humanities, and Health Sciences.

fields.name: Sol Plaatje University
fields.description: Sol Plaatje University (SPU) is a young comprehensive university in Kimberley, Northern Cape, established in 2014. It offers undergraduate programmes across Education, Humanities, Economic & Management Sciences, and Natural & Applied Sciences, with a strong focus on serving the Northern Cape community.

fields.name: University of Zululand
fields.description: The University of Zululand (UniZulu) is a comprehensive university with its main campus in KwaDlangezwa and a second campus in Richards Bay, KwaZulu-Natal. It offers programmes across Humanities & Social Sciences, Commerce, Administration & Law, Education, and Science, Agriculture & Engineering.

fields.name: University of Limpopo
fields.description: The University of Limpopo (UL) is a comprehensive university located at Turfloop Campus in Sovenga, near Polokwane, Limpopo. It offers programmes across Health Sciences (including Medicine), Humanities, Management & Law, and Science & Agriculture, serving predominantly rural communities in Limpopo and neighbouring provinces.

fields.name: University of Mpumalanga
fields.description: The University of Mpumalanga (UMP) is a young comprehensive university established in 2014, with campuses in Mbombela and Siyabuswa, Mpumalanga. It offers programmes in Agriculture & Natural Sciences, Economics & Business, Education, and ICT, with a strong focus on the needs of the Mpumalanga region.

fields.name: Central University of Technology
fields.description: The Central University of Technology (CUT) is a university of technology in Bloemfontein, Free State, with a second campus in Welkom. It offers programmes in Engineering & IT, Health & Environmental Sciences, Humanities, and Management Sciences, with a strong applied and vocational focus.

fields.name: University of Venda
fields.description: The University of Venda (UNIVEN) is a comprehensive university located in Thohoyandou, Limpopo. It serves the Vhembe district and surrounding communities, offering programmes in Science, Engineering & Agriculture, Health Sciences, Management, Commerce & Law, and Humanities, Social Sciences & Education.

fields.name: Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University
fields.description: Sefako Makgatho Health Sciences University (SMU), formerly known as MEDUNSA, is a specialised health sciences university located in Ga-Rankuwa, Gauteng. It offers programmes in Medicine, Dentistry, Pharmacy, Nursing, Allied Health Sciences, and Science & Technology, with a strong focus on training health professionals for underserved communities in South Africa.

fields.name: University of South Africa
fields.description: The University of South Africa (UNISA) is Africa's largest open distance e-learning (ODeL) university, offering over 300 undergraduate and postgraduate qualifications across 7 colleges. With no APS gate, admission is based on your NSC endorsement type (Higher Certificate, Diploma, or Bachelor's). UNISA serves over 400,000 students and is fully accredited for NSFAS funding.

fields.name: Buffalo City TVET College
fields.description: Buffalo City TVET College is a public TVET college based in East London, Eastern Cape. It is accredited by the DHET and NSFAS-approved, offering NCV (National Certificate Vocational, NQF Levels 2–4) and NATED (Report 191, N1–N6) programmes in engineering, business studies, and vocational trades. No APS is required — entry is by Grade 9 or Grade 12 pass depending on the programme.

Career opportunities exist in public and private emergency services, both local and abroad. Holders of the qualification will be entitled to regis.

Career opportunities exist in public and private emergency services, both local and abroad. Holders of the qualification will be entitled to register as independent Emergency Care Practitioners with the HPCSA.

fields.name: Bachelor of Optometry in B Optom
fields.description: 4-year Bachelor of Optometry in B Optom programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Doornfontein Campus. Career paths include: Graduates can pursue careers as optometrists providing primary eye care, specialising in eyes examination to identify and address a spectrum of visual related irregularities, such as vision defects, abnormalities related to injury or ocular disease. Beyond general patient care, other areas include p.
fields.career_opportunities: Graduates can pursue careers as optometrists providing primary eye care, specialising in eyes examination to identify and address a spectrum of visual related irregularities, such as vision defects, abnormalities related to injury or ocular disease. Beyond general patient care, other areas include paediatric optometry, low vision or contact lens fitting. Optometrists can engage in research, academia, hospital settings or public health initiatives, private practise, corporate optometry and industry consulting.

Career paths include: Scientist in the field of food and water, and biomedical, biotechnological and agricultural sectors..
fields.career_opportunities: Scientist in the field of food and water, and biomedical, biotechnological and agricultural sectors.

fields.name: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Chemistry And Mathematics
fields.description: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Chemistry And Mathematics programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Auckland Park Kingsway. Career paths include: Chemistry, Researcher, Technologist, Problem Solver..
fields.career_opportunities: Chemistry, Researcher, Technologist, Problem Solver.

fields.name: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Chemistry And Physics
fields.description: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Chemistry And Physics programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Auckland Park Kingsway. Career paths include: Physicist, Researcher, Chemistry Technologist, Forensic Researcher..
fields.career_opportunities: Physicist, Researcher, Chemistry Technologist, Forensic Researcher.

fields.name: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Physics And Applied Mathematics
fields.description: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Physics And Applied Mathematics programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Auckland Park Kingsway. Career paths include: Problem Solver, Researcher in Astronomy and Energy Sources..
fields.career_opportunities: Problem Solver, Researcher in Astronomy and Energy Sources.

fields.name: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Physics And Mathematics
fields.description: Bachelor of Science in Physical Sciences (Extended) in Physics And Mathematics programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Auckland Park Kingsway. Career paths include: Problem Solver, Researcher in Astronomy and Energy Sources..
fields.career_opportunities: Problem Solver, Researcher in Astronomy and Energy Sources.

fields.name: Diploma (Extended) Analytical Chemistry
fields.description: Diploma (Extended) Analytical Chemistry programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Doornfontein Campus. Career paths include: Analytical Chemists analyse raw materials and final products to gauge their quality levels..
fields.career_opportunities: Analytical Chemists analyse raw materials and final products to gauge their quality levels.

fields.name: Diploma (Extended) Biotechnology
fields.description: Diploma (Extended) Biotechnology programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Doornfontein Campus. Career paths include: Biotechnologists manipulate living cells for application in the baking, brewing, chemical, agricultural and pharmaceutical fields..
fields.career_opportunities: Biotechnologists manipulate living cells for application in the baking, brewing, chemical, agricultural and pharmaceutical fields.

fields.name: Diploma (Extended) Food Technology
fields.description: Diploma (Extended) Food Technology programme at the University of Johannesburg. Offered at Doornfontein Campus. Career paths include: Food Technologists research the production, processing, preservation, packing and distribution of food..
fields.career_opportunities: Food Technologists research the production, processing, preservation, packing and distribution of food.

fields.name: Higher Certificate in Sports Sciences
fields.description: The Higher Certificate in Sports Sciences aims to develop sports coaches and administrators who can function successfully in an interdisciplinary environment. This qualification equips students with the practical skills to provide physical education through sports programmes and promote principles of good management in sports in order to improve the performance of athletes and sports teams. The qualification further provides students with solid foundational knowledge, cognitive and practical skills required for higher education studies in sports sciences and education. Who is the ideal candidate? The ideal candidate holds a certificate recognised by the University as equivalent to the National Senior Certificate (NSC), with an Admission Point Score (APS) of 20. Students who are passionate about sports and physical education excel in this programme. It is the ideal programme for student athletes and professional sports people who want to further their studies while competing in sporting events. What makes this programme unique? Many young people who have proven sports talents and the ability to learn at an advanced level, but do not meet the entry requirements for study at a diploma or degree level, develop those talents into learning and career pathways. Very few schools in South Africa have qualified sports teachers. The Higher Certificate serves as a critical entry point into higher education training as the first step to possible career pathways in sports coaching, administration or teaching.
fields.career_opportunities: Sports coaching, role in sports and exercise industry

fields.name: AdvDip in School Leadership and Management (Distance Education)
fields.description: This programme will be presented in distance and hybrid off-campus modes. The programme extends over one or two years and is presented with two optional contact sessions per year, followed by ICT-driven guided study.

fields.name: AdvDip in Visual Impairment Studies (Distance Education)
fields.description: This advanced diploma in Education underscores the importance of visual impairment education for teachers and practitioners who teach and collaborate with learners with a visual impairment. The qualification focuses on understanding visual impairment, in terms of low vision and blindness. The aim is to equip teachers and practitioners, in support of learners with a visual impairment and also to enhance ongoing research in this field.

fields.name: Diploma in Theology
fields.description: The Diploma in Theology offers general theological education to students who have completed the NSC/IEB (or equivalent qualification), but without the required university admission. It also provides a basis for denominational programmes, formation and practical training with a view to the ministry. People study Theology and Religion for various reasons, and not only to make it their profession. Some find it to be a fascinating field that they would like to explore, whereas others search for spiritual growth through study, or may want to gain insights that will help them to function more effectively in the workplace. The Faculty also offers programmes that will benefit students who do not necessarily want to become ministers, but would like to enrich their lives and faith experiences. The Theology and Religion programme deals with the fundamental questions of life: How do faith and religion influence peopleâs lives and society? How does one approach ethical and moral questions in the workplace? How do theological insights promote good human resource practices? How does one cultivate ecological sensitivity and awareness? How do Christians relate to laws? How do religious values contribute to peace and good human relations? Can crime be stopped by appealing to peopleâs beliefs and values? What is the meaning and purpose of life? Where does one find answers to the most profound life questions? How does one gain a better understanding of humanity to make a difference in the world? What kind of leaders are needed to shape society and the world? In the case of students who feel called to work in churches as ministers, pastors, priests, lay preachers,  missionaries or youth workers, studying Theology and Religion is the way to go. Others find their calling in serving the community as social workers, development workers, chaplains, psychologists, teachers, journalists or writers, or become engineers, lawyers or entrepreneurs, but regardless of the professions people choo
fields.career_opportunities: Ministers, pastors, priests, lay preachers in certain denominations, missionaries and youth counsellors. Candidates should consult their church leadership on which programme would best suit their aims. The Diploma in Theology is intended for candidates who have completed NSC/IEB (or equivalent), but without the required university admission.

fields.name: Bachelor of Information Science [BIS]
fields.description: Information and technology are found in every aspect of modern society, and there is a growing need for graduates who understand how people interact with information and technology. This is especially relevant with new technologies from the Fourth Industrial Revolution and other innovations. This degree focuses on using technology to manage and process information products. Students learn how to organise, retrieve and share information, as well as how to make it more useful and valuable. Students also gain knowledge in managing information and knowledge, which are the most critical resources for any organisation. This includes learning about knowledge management, competitive intelligence and digital tools like online repositories.
fields.career_opportunities: Knowledge managers (manage information and knowledge resources)Information specialists (organise, retrieve and add value to information)Information consultants (consult on information products, services and systems)Information brokers (become an infopreneur and buy and sell information products and services)Systems specialists (analyse and develop information systems)

fields.name: Bachelor of Information Science specialising in Multimedia [BIS]
fields.description: The rapid evolution of digital technology has redefined how we create, communicate and interact with information. The Multimedia qualification in the Department of Information Science equips students to design and develop interactive digital experiences across various platforms and media. This includes websites, games, animations, mobile applications and other user-centred interactive systems. The programme integrates technical development skills with creative problem-solving and critical understanding of current computing and digital innovation trends. Students gain hands-on experience in web development, interaction design, animation, video and sound editing and 3D modelling. A strong emphasis is placed on programming and core computer science principles, giving students the foundation to build robust, scalable and intelligent digital systems. They also engage with human-computer interaction, user experience design and emerging technology trends. By combining practical application with theoretical insight, the qualification prepares students to navigate and shape the rapidly changing landscape of interactive technology. This degree offers the skills and perspective needed to remain relevant and adaptable in an increasingly digital world where engagement, usability and innovation are critical. A student with this degree will work in a team of developers and designers and communicate easily with both groups. They will also have the skills to move between these two types of roles within a company. They will also be able to further their understanding of design, animation, and game design and development and then work in those fields. The ideal candidate should be: passionate about computing and technological advancements; happy to spend many hours in front of a computer; interested in creating and maintaining websites  (both front- and back-end); interested in learning about animation, image, sound and  video editing; interested in the intersection between technical
fields.career_opportunities: Full-stack developer, programme/software developer, front-end designer, interactive technology content creator, UI/UX designer/developer, and back-end developer

fields.name: Bachelor of Information Science specialising in Publishing [BIS]
fields.description: The Bachelor of Information Science specialising in Publishing programme teaches publishing theory and skills by selecting and developing content based on the needs of the user and appropriately packaging this content through a process of adding value. Publishing can happen in both paper-based and electronic format and includes a range of products, such as books for the trade market and publications for educational, academic and corporate readers. Publishing processes are also used in the production of mass media products, such as newspapers and magazines. This programme aims to: provide students with knowledge of the publishing process and role-players, as well as trends and initiatives in the local and international publishing industry; provide students with relevant and current skills, including editing, design and production; enable students to work with a variety of information formats, from print to digital; and make students aware of the social, ethical and legal responsibilities involved in the publishing process. The ideal candidate should have: excellent language and communication skills, both written and verbal; project management and the ability to work  towards goals and deadlines; computer skills and an aptitude for learning new skills; critical reading and reasoning; and good business sense.
fields.career_opportunities: Graduates can work in a wide variety of publishing, communication and media functions. Various career opportunities are available in the publishing industry, book retail and corporate communications. Content production for media houses, magazines and  other content creators is also possible. Some career opportunities include the following:   Editorial functions Layout, design and typesettingDigital production Copyright permissions and negotiationsMarketing and promotionSelf-publishing and consultancy

fields.name: Bachelor of Town and Regional Planning [Btrp]
fields.description: Town and Regional Planning prepares students for a career in which they help plan, design and manage changes in society through the development and use of land. Students learn to create projects and interventions at different levelsâfrom designing individual houses or commercial sites to planning entire neighbourhoods, cities or even regions. The aim is to give people more choices in where and how they live, promote fairness and equal access to resources, support environmentally and socially sustainable communities and improve overall quality of life. The main goal of this profession is to find innovative, fair, sustainable and affordable ways to improve how people live and use land. In South Africa today, town and regional planners play an important role in fixing imbalances in cities and rural areas, and in improving settlements that are poorly planned, unfair or inefficient. Planning is challenging because different people and groupsâsuch as local communities, businesses, government and environmental organisationsâoften have different and sometimes conflicting ideas about what should happen in a town or region. Planners must find solutions that balance these interests while improving the area for everyone. Because South Africa faces big challenges like housing shortages, limited social services and high levels of poverty and inequality, planners need a strong sense of social and environmental justice. They should be committed to improving the lives of people and communities. The professional four-year Bachelor of Town and Regional Planning qualification enables graduates to register as professional town and regional planners with the South African Council for Planners (SACPLAN), which is an official body established in terms of an Act of Parliament. The degree is internationally recognised.
fields.career_opportunities: While most town and regional planners are employed in the three spheres of government, or act as private consultants to the public and the private sectors, they are also employed by research agencies such as the Council for Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR) and the Human Sciences Research Council (HSRC), non-governmental and development organisations, community-based organisations, major financial institutions and property development groups.

fields.name: BEng in Mechanical Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Mechanical engineering is concerned with the design, manufacture and operation of components, devices or systems such as: Thermal power systems, heat exchangers, cooling and refrigeration systems, ventilation systems, etc. Manufacturing technologies that include the use of lasers, precision machinery Automobiles, aeroplanes and other vehicles Robotics, mechatronics and electronic control of machinery Digitisation of physical assets through sensing, computing and data science The undergraduate programme gives students a broad foundation in engineering. It covers topics such as dynamics, strength of materials, thermodynamics, fluid mechanics and design. Students learn the mechanical and aeronautical skills needed to design and make products and services, including electricity and water systems, transport (road, rail and air), mining, mechatronics and air conditioning.
fields.career_opportunities: Mechanical engineers are employed in almost all sectors of the economy, including the chemical industry, mining, manufacturing, processing, vehicle and aircraft design, defence and aeronautics. Possible careers include Aerospace engineer, Automotive engineer, Maintenance engineer, Design engineer, Mathematical and computational modeling engineer, and Data science engineer.

fields.name: BEng in Industrial Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Industrial engineers focus on productivity by designing, testing, planning and managing systems that bring together people, equipment, materials, information and energy. They work to make these systems more efficient and effective. Industrial engineers also combine the work of other engineering fields to create a final, functional product or service that is both functional and marketable. The demand for industrial engineers currently exceeds the supply, and graduates are virtually assured of employment. What industrial engineers do: Design and manage production processes and equipment Improve factory and workplace layouts Make business processes more effective Create and use information systems Set up and apply performance standards Help with decision-making Plan and schedule work activities Analyse systems using mathsl and simulations Compare costs and benefits of different options Fit new systems into existing ones The ideal candidate is someone with: Problem-solving skills Critical thinking skills Project management skills Communication and organisation skills
fields.career_opportunities: Industrial engineers design, test, implement and manage a wide range of man/machine systems for production and the delivery of services. Organisational matters that require optimisation include site selection and layout of facilities, manufacturing, inventory control, materials handling, supply chain management, quality management, cost control, financial services, maintenance, reliability, computer simulation, information systems, human resources and business law.

fields.name: BEng in Chemical Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: A chemical engineer, also known as a process engineer,  finds ways to convert cheap raw materials into more valuable products. Theoretical knowledge of chemistry is required, but the field mostly focuses on the application of intense mathematics to make processes as efficient as possible. The programme provides students with the necessary foundation to ensure that once they have graduated, they will be able to make creative contributions to the worldâs ever-increasing needs by: Converting natural resources into efficient and useable forms of energy Developing more durable, lighter and renewable materials Designing more efficient, environmentally friendly processing plants Applying biotechnology to convert raw materials into products in a sustainable way Designing processes to ensure that limited natural resources, such as water, can be reused Leaving a clean and sustainable environment behind for future generations. The programme builds a solid foundation in chemistry, physics, mathematics, and biology, together with the principles of mass, energy, and momentum conservation. Students then apply these concepts alongside economic principles to design equipment and processes that are efficient and profitable, supporting industrial and economic growth. The goal is to produce graduates who can create new and innovative processes to meet future needs.
fields.career_opportunities: Chemical engineers work in industrial processes that turn raw materials into higher-value products. They apply knowledge in fields such as petroleum, food, minerals processing, power generation, paper and pulp, water and effluent treatment and environmental engineering (e.g. air pollution control). They are involved in research and development, process design and optimisation, equipment and plant design, construction, commissioning, operation, and management. They may also work in marketing and distribution of products.

fields.name: BEng in Civil Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Civil engineers create facilities that improve peopleâs lives and the environment. Their work includes research, planning, design, construction and ongoing maintenance of various structures. Civil engineers design, build and maintain constructions such as tower blocks and skyscrapers, dams, canals and pipelines, roads, bridges, tunnels, railway lines, airports, power stations, towers, waterworks and outfall installations. Because these facilities have long lifespans and affect communities and the environment, civil engineers are trained to handle analytical design tasks, consult directly with communities and stakeholders, and ensure projects are cost-effectively and beneficial to society. . Modern information technology and computer software have changed civil engineering by enabling continuous data collection, mathematical modelling and more efficient design. As a result, civil engineers can focus on the fundamental aspects of developmental work and design. Environmental engineering and management are now key parts of training, providing graduates with a broad-based qualification that offers challenging and adaptable career opportunities over a career span of 40 - 50 years.
fields.career_opportunities: Civil engineers design, build and maintain constructions such as tower blocks and skyscrapers, dams, canals and pipelines, roads, bridges, tunnels, railways, airports, power stations, towers, waterworks and outfall installations. They can find work in any company involved in development, design, construction and management of infrastructure and related services.

fields.name: BEng in Computer Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Computer engineering is one of the fastest-growing engineering fields within information and communication technology (ICT). Almost every modern system in the world depends on it. This field combines electronics, computer hardware and software and communication systems. A computer engineer is someone who improves electronic systems by adding computing power and control software. Their work ranges from small microcontroller networks to the worldwide web. Computer engineers need a strong foundation in science and a solid education in both theory and practice. This includes electronics, digital systems, computer systems and control software. With today's rapid growth in computing power and storage, and the drop in cost and size of technology, computer engineering now plays a role in nearly every modern most system. Computer engineering is used in the following fields: telecommunications, computer networking, cell phone operations, computer system companies, military technologies (avionics, night vision, electronic warfare, drones), transport technologies, internet banking, security systems, consumer equipment, modems, handheld scanners, voting, medical systems (portable and remote diagnostic recorders), robotics, entertainment equipment, global positioning system (GPS), navigation, measurement and control software, and fibre-optic (self-healing networks).
fields.career_opportunities: Computer engineering graduates have access to a wide range of job opportunities, which include working for a company (large or small) anywhere in the world, being an entrepreneur or being self-employed. Research and development opportunities are available in the fields of communication, computer systems, networking and peace-keeping operations, and in medical, transportation, software and electronics companies in South Africa and all over the world.

fields.name: BEng in Electrical Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Electrical engineering is prevalent in almost all application fields and technologies where electrical energy is consumed. Every known piece of equipment requires a source of energyâpowered by mains, batteries or photovoltaic (PV) cellsâand needs the skill of an electrical engineer. The transport and manufacturing industries are excellent examples of industries in which electrical engineers use their superior skills to design, develop and maintain electrical machines (motors and generators) with control systems for optimal performance. Most ships and trains are electrically powered. Electrical engineers also work on power supply systems in cities, townships, malls and factories. They design and manage lighting for both indoor and outdoor spaces, such as sports stadiums, streets, offices, and even safety and even security and entertainment areas. No matter the fieldâwhether it is medicine, the military, entertainment, sports or educationâelectrical engineers play a role in providing the energy and control systems that keep things running. The goal of electrical engineering is to find new and sustainable ways to generate, transmit, and use energy to improve the world. Examples of subsystems that are part of larger electrical systems include: Electrical machines of all sizes and shapes Power electronics Control systems power system components Power quality and network stability Lamps and lighting Power supplies Photovoltaic (PV) cells Solar geysers Space systems Robotics and energy management systems.
fields.career_opportunities: Electrical engineering is prevalent in almost all application fields and technologies where electrical energy is consumed. Every known piece of equipment requires a source of energyâpowered by mains, batteries or photovoltaic (PV) cellsâand needs the skill of an electrical engineer. The transport and manufacturing industries are excellent examples of industries in which electrical engineers use their superior skills to design, develop and maintain electrical machines (motors and generators) with control systems for optimal performance. Most ships and trains are electrically powered. Electrical engineers also work on power supply systems in cities, townships, malls and factories. They design and manage lighting for both indoor and outdoor spaces, such as sports stadiums, streets, offices and even security and entertainment areas.No matter the fieldâwhether it is medicine, the military, entertainment, sports or educationâelectrical engineers play a role in providing the energy and control systems that keep things running. The goal of electrical engineering is to find new and sustainable ways to generate, transmit and use energy to improve the world.

fields.name: BEng in Electronic Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: Electronic engineering is a fast-growing field that shapes almost every part of modern life. Nearly all technology today depends on electronics in some way. Electronic engineers play a key role in creating new technologies and improving existing ones. To do this work, they need a strong foundation in science as well as both theoretical knowledge and practical skills, such as design. With the rapid growth of new electronic systems around the world, electronic engineers must be ready to adapt and meet industry needs. Electronic engineering is an exciting field that is always changing. Because technologies like microelectronics and photonics improve so quickly, new advances happen about every two-and-a-half years. The goal of electronic engineering is to make things work faster, cost less, be smaller in size and give better control. Typical subsystems that form part of larger electronic systems include: Amplifiers Transmitters and receivers Control and sensor systems Antennas Power supplies Radio frequency (RF) subsystems Micro and nanoelectronics and microprocessors Digital signal processors (DSPs)
fields.career_opportunities: Electronic engineering graduates have access to a wide range of job opportunities, which include working for companies (large or small) anywhere in the world as employees, or being entrepreneurs or selfemployed. Research and development opportunities are available at South African electronics and microelectronics companies and research institutes (such as the CSIR), and at universities all over the world. Graduates in electronic engineering have the opportunity to be innovative, e.g. to identify real-life problems and to come up with solutions, which they might be able to patent. From 2026, three options for specialisation are presented for a postgraduate qualification: Biomedical Engineering, Robotics and Automation.

fields.name: BEng in Metallurgical Engineering 4-year programme
fields.description: South Africa is rich in minerals, with the world's largest deposits of platinum, chromium and manganese, as well as significant reserves of gold, iron, copper, nickel, coal, vanadium and diamonds. The minerals industry is a major part of the economy, contributing nearly half of the countryâs exports and providing a wide range of job opportunities. Metallurgical engineers play a key role in turning these raw materials into useful metals and products. Their work supports both local and international markets and ensures that metal-based products, from cars to electronics, work safely and effectively in everyday life. The three main fields of specialisation in metallurgical engineering: Minerals processing. Processing the ore to release and concentrate the valuable minerals contained in it. Extractive metallurgy. The processing of mineral concentrates to metals through pyrometallurgy (including smelting) or hydrometallurgy (including leaching) as refining steps. Materials production, performance and integrity. This field involves developing new metal alloys and turning raw metals into useful products. Metals can be shaped through casting, 3D printing (using lasers or wire arc methods) and welding. An important part of the work is investigating why materials fail âsuch as when a metal component cracks, breaks or doesn't perform as expectedâto prevent problems in real products.
fields.career_opportunities: Careers in this field include roles such as production engineers, plant managers, consultants, researchers and specialists in areas such as materials, corrosion, welding, nuclear and forensic engineering. Other specialisation areas include minerals processing, extractive metallurgy, materials performance and advanced manufacturing.

fields.name: BEng in Industrial Engineering 5-year programme
fields.description: Industrial engineers focus on productivity by designing, testing, planning and managing systems that bring together people, equipment, materials, information and energy. They work to make these systems more efficient and effective. Industrial engineers also combine the work of other engineering fields to create a final, functional product or service that is both functional and marketable. The demand for industrial engineers currently exceeds the supply, and graduates are virtually assured of employment. They may also work in marketing and distribution of products.

With the rapid growth of new electronic systems around the world, electronic engineers must be ready to adapt and meet industry needs. Electronic engineering is an exciting field that is always changing. Because technologies like microelectronics and photonics improve so quickly, new advances happen about every two-and-a-half years. The goal of electronic engineering is to make things work faster, cost less, be smaller in size and give better control. Typical subsystems that form part of larger electronic systems include: Amplifiers Transmitters and receivers Control and sensor systems Antennas Power supplies Radio frequency (RF) subsystems Micro and nanoelectronics and microprocessors Digital signal processors (DSPs)
fields.career_opportunities: Electronic engineering graduates have access to a wide range of job opportunities, which include working for companies (large or small) anywhere in the world as employees, or being entrepreneurs or selfemployed. Research and development opportunities are available at South African electronics and microelectronics companies and research institutes (such as the CSIR), and at universities all over the world. Graduates in electronic engineering have the opportunity to be innovative, e.g. to identify real-life problems and to come up with solutions, which they might be able to patent. From 2026, three options for specialisation are presented for a postgraduate qualification: Biomedical Engineering, Robotics and Automation.

fields.name: BEng in Computer Engineering 5-year programme
fields.description: Computer engineering is one of the fastest-growing engineering fields within information and communication technology (ICT). Almost every modern system in the world depends on it. This field combines electronics, computer hardware and software and communication systems. A computer engineer is someone who improves electronic systems by adding computing power and control software. Their work ranges from small microcontroller networks to the worldwide web. Computer engineers need a strong foundation in science and a solid education in both theory and practice. This includes electronics, digital systems, computer systems and control software. With today's rapid growth in computing power and storage, and the drop in cost and size of technology, computer engineering now plays a role in nearly every modern most system. Computer engineering is used in the following fields: telecommunications, computer networking, cell phone operations, computer system companies, military technologies (avionics, night vision, electronic warfare, drones), transport technologies, internet banking, security systems, consumer equipment, modems, handheld scanners, voting, medical systems (portable and remote diagnostic recorders), robotics, entertainment equipment, global positioning system (GPS), navigation, measurement and control software, and fibre-optic (self-healing networks).
fields.career_opportunities: Computer engineering graduates have access to a wide range of job opportunities, which include working for a company (large or small) anywhere in the world, being an entrepreneur or being self-employed. Research and development opportunities are available in the fields of communication, computer systems, networking and peace-keeping operations, and in medical, transportation, software and electronics companies in South Africa and all over the world.

fields.name: Bachelor of Information Technology in Information Systems [BIT]
fields.description: Students who enrol for this programme study how computer and information systems are used in organisations. As the use of information technology grows rapidly, new and more complex applications are constantly being developed. Informatics specialists learn to analyse business problems and improve the efficiency, effectiveness and control of processes in commercial organisations, government departments, non-profit organisations or any other organisation where information is important. They not only identify business needs but also design and implement information systems to meet them. Today, the term 'information systems' usually refers to computer-based systems, including mobile applications, that store and manage data so people can understand it and make better decisions.
fields.career_opportunities: Informatics specialists can choose between many excellent job opportunities, for example:Business analystSystem analystData scientistKnowledge managerQuality assurance testerUser experience designerProject managerDeveloper (front end, back end or full stack)IT auditorIT entrepreneurIT tax specialiste-business consultant

fields.name: BEd in Early Childhood Care and Education
fields.description: The Bachelor of Education in Early Childhood Care and Education (ECCE) prepares students to teach and support young children from birth to 4 years old. The programme focuses on childrenâs care, development, and learning in the early years. Admission requires an appropriate combination of recognised NSC subjects and achievement levels. This programme is ideal for students who are passionate about working with young children and supporting their development. It is suited to those who want to become registered and qualified ECCE educators. Early Childhood Development is a key priority for social and economic growth in South Africa . University of Pretoria is among the first institutions to offer a professional degree specifically focused on educators working with children from birth to 4 years old. Alongside the Foundation Phase Teaching programme, this degree strengthens the Universityâs contribution to the ECD sector. The degree equips students with disciplinary knowledge, educational theory, and practical teaching skills. Graduates are prepared to become competent, responsible ECCE educators who can make a meaningful impact in the lives of young children.
fields.career_opportunities: Teachers and training officials at primary or secondary schools.

fields.name: BEd in Foundation Phase Teaching
fields.description: The Bachelor of Education in Foundation Phase Teaching programme trains students to teach young children between the ages of three and nine years. Students who complete this programme specialise in Foundation Phase (Grades R to 3). Admission requires an appropriate combination of recognised NSC subjects and the required achievement levels in those subjects. Students who are compassionate, hardworking, and committed to the teaching profession excel in this programme. The Bachelor of Education in Foundation Phase Teaching degree programme is accredited. Students who have completed this degree programme possess diverse pedagogic skills that enable them to teach different learning areas (Home Language, First Additional Language, Mathematics, and Life Skills) and differentiate the curriculum for access by learners in schools. Furthermore, the programme equips the student with the unique skills and attributes required to identify learning difficulties during the formational stages of development and provide interventions for learning success.
fields.career_opportunities: Teachers and training officials at pre-primary or primary schools

fields.name: BEd in Senior Phase and Further Education and Training Teaching
fields.description: The purpose of this programme is to train students to teach in the Senior Phase and Further Education and Training Teaching (FET) phase. Apart from the Core and Fundamental subjects that are prescribed for this programme, students can choose electives (from a prescribed list) according to the following rules: Three subjects are to be taken in the first year, two are taken to second year and one to third-year level.There are exceptions to the rule, especially in Mathematics and Sciences. The exceptions are clearly described in the Faculty of Education yearbook. Two electives for FET teaching and one elective for Senior Phase (SP) teaching or one elective for FET teaching and two electives for SP teaching. The ideal candidate is someone who has passed matric and is passionate about teaching. Successful students are dedicated, hardworking, and committed to teaching and learning. Students who excel in this programme: are willing to work hard and independently; take responsibility for their studies; are prepared to ask for help when needed; are prepared to make responsible choices; and believe in teaching as a profession and a calling. The programme has SAQA accreditation and qualifies a successful student as both a subject and a teaching specialist. Through Work Integrated Learning, the student gets practical experience in schools, giving them the confidence to stand as a well-equipped professional in front of a class from their first day.
fields.career_opportunities: Teachers and training officials at primary or secondary schools.

fields.name: BEd in Intermediate Phase Teaching
fields.description: The Bachelor of Education in Intermediate Phase Teaching programme consists of fundamental, core and elective modules that prepare students to teach effectively in multilingual and multicultural classrooms. Students who are passionate about how children learn and grow are well suited to this programme. They are caring, motivated, and interested in creating supportive learning environments. A strong work ethic, commitment to the teaching profession, and enthusiasm for working with diverse learners will help students succeed in this programme. The Bachelor of Education in Intermediate Phase Teaching degree programme qualifies successful candidates to teach a language and another subject/learning area. Language is the primary medium of communication and instruction in the classroom, and this programme therefore places strong emphasis on language education. The language elective equips future teachers to communicate effectively and deliver quality teaching across diverse classroom settings.
fields.career_opportunities: Teachers and training officials at pre-primary or primary schools.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Econometrics
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Econometrics is a three-year full-time programme that introduces students to economic theory, economic policy, and the statistical foundations of empirical analysis, commonly referred to as econometrics. For professionals, econometrics is a fascinating field that enables the measurement and analysis of economic phenomena and the forecasting of future economic trends. It focuses on understanding economic behaviour, outcomes, and the impact of policies through the integration of economic theory, data, and statistical techniques. Econometricians are typically statistically and mathematically inclined, and may specialise in areas such as: Statistical analysis of economic and financial markets Forecasting Structural analysis Policy analysis Econometricians are employed across a wide range of sectors, including: Government The South African Reserve Bank National Treasury Private and commercial banks Stock brokerage firms Consultancies, both locally and internationally. Career paths include roles as analysts, consultants, researchers, and academics. This programme is ideal for students who are logical and analytical, curious and creative, and motivated and open-minded. Visit www.up.ac.za/economics for more information.
fields.career_opportunities: Econometricians are employed by government, the central bank, private and commercial banks, leading stockbrokers and consultancies, both locally and internationally. Positions include analysts, consultants, researchers, traders or brokers, and academics. The work mostly entails statistical analysis (forecasting, structural and policy analysis) of economic and financial markets and interrelationships.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Economics
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Economics is a three-year full-time programme that introduces students to economic theory, economic policy, and the empirical issues associated with economic analysis. A career in economics involves uncovering and forecasting economic trends, as well as analysing the effects of policy changes on society and the global economy. Economists may specialise in areas such as: Banking Public finance International trade and investment Economic development Economists are employed by: Government The South African Reserve Bank National Treasury Private and commercial banks Stock brokerage firms Consultancies, both locally and internationally. Career paths include roles as analysts, consultants, researchers, and academics. This programme is ideal for students who are logical and analytical, curious and creative, and motivated and open-minded. Visit www.up.ac.za/economics for more information.
fields.career_opportunities: Economists may specialise in banking, public finance, international trade and investment or economic development. They are employed by the government, the central bank, private and commercial banks, stock brokerage firms and consultancies, both locally and internationally. Positions include analysts, consultants, researchers, traders and academics.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Supply Chain Management
fields.description: Supply chain management involves managing the entire flow of materials, information, services and money, from raw materials suppliers to factories, warehouses and ultimately to the end users. The Bachelor of Commerce in Supply Chain Management is a threeâyear degree that prepares students to manage all parts of a supply chain in a coordinated way. The programme covers: Sourcing and supply management Production and operations management Warehousing management Transport management Supply chain strategy The Department of Business Management at UP is the only European Logistics Association (ELA) National Certification Centre (NCC) in Sub-Saharan Africa. This degree is accredited at Level 4: Candidate European Junior Logistician (cEJLog), which is based on the European Logistics Association Qualifications Framework Standards agreed by industry. ELA candidates are awarded a certificate that is a global mark of competence and promotes mobility in an international marketplace. This qualification offers graduates a smooth path to full ELA certification once the relevant experience has been is gained. The ideal candidates for this programme are: Globally minded and culturally aware Analytical and a strong problem-solvers Innovative, creative and adaptable Natural leaders and effective team players Technically savvy with strong business and communication skills Curious and eager to learn Our graduates are employed by: Transportation companies Retail companies that sell items such as clothing, food and technology products directly to customers Manufacturing companies Purchasing departments of various organisations Enquiries: Please send an email to click here
fields.career_opportunities: The purpose of this programme is to equip students with the ability to participate in functional management within an integrated supply chain. This includes supply management, production and operations management, warehousing, transport management and supply chain strategy.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Business Management
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce in Business Management is a threeâyear degree that helps students understand the big picture of how businesses work. It shows how all parts of an organisation connect, while teaching important skills such as critical thinking, problemâsolving, and decisionâmaking. These skills are taught using both South African and international best practices. Students benefit from visits by industry experts and practical simulations, which bring realâworld experience into the classroom. This degree combines theory with practice, ensuring that graduates are ready for the workplace and have the knowledge to start and manage their own businesses. Students who do well in Business Management are usually: Able to see the big picture Creative thinkers Analytical thinkers Self-starters Able to integrate ideas and concepts Systematic organisers and planners The career prospectives of graduates who complete this degree are vast. They could either join established businesses or start their own. Enquiries: Please send an email to Prof AJ Antonites [email protected] For more information: www.up.ac.za/business-management
fields.career_opportunities: A wide variety of opportunities exist in the areas of administration, finance, marketing and human resources management, including employment as teachers, lecturers, general managers and management consultants

fields.name: BCom specialising in Human Resource Management
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Human Resource Management degree introduces students to the key disciplines of: Industrial and Organisational Psychology: Using knowledge of the psychology of human behaviour to ensure that people work âbetterâ in the workplace. Human Resource Management: Managing people using workplace policies and procedures to ensure that they are effective and productive. Labour Relations Management: Implementing Labour Relations programmes and acting as mediators through advising and counselling in the workplace. This programme is closely aligned with the 13 standards of human resource (HR) practice set by the South African Board of People Practices (SABPP), the professional body for HR practitioners in South Africa. This means students gain skills and knowledge that are industryârelevant and match the changing needs of the HR profession. Each module is designed to highlight specific SABPP standards and competencies that HR professionals need to add value in the workplace. Accreditation The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Human Resource Management degree is accredited at the South African Board for People Practitioners (SABPP). Students who excel in this programme are able to: Communicate effectively Solve problems Comfortably make difficult decisions Multi-task Show kindness and understanding Maintain strong ethical principles Almost every company has a Human Resource (HR) division, which means graduates are in demand across industries. With experience, graduates can also start their own companies offering HR services in their chosen specialisation. For more information visit https://www.up.ac.za/human-resource-management
fields.career_opportunities: Human resources practitioners, human resources consultants, mediators, labour relations specialists, human resources managers, personnel managers, training officers, liaison officers, psychometrists and industrial psychologists. In their capacity as human resources practitioners, graduates will be responsible for matters such as developing human resource strategies and policies, human resource planning, recruitment and selection of employees, training and development of staff, labour relations and personnel administration.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Law
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Law degree is a three-year programme that focuses on core economic and management sciences subjects, but also includes some law modules. It provides a broader perspective on issues such as taxation and commercial law for those who wish to practise law in these fields. It offers an alternate route to the eventual attainment of a Bachelor of Laws degree [also referred to as LLB], which is the minimum requirement for entering the organised legal profession. Graduates may also specialise in a field in the economic and management sciences instead of law, in which case they would register for an honours degree in the Faculty of Economic and Management Sciences. This programme caters for students who have a dual interest in business and law. It offers a combination of commerce and law subjects to prepare students for the law profession, but with a broad commercial academic background. It enables a graduate to acquire a Bachelor of Laws [also referred to as LLB] within two years after completion of the Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Law degree. Graduates have expertise in both economic and legal fields, which enhances their employability. The programme is jointly presented with the UP Faculty of Law. To excel in this programme, prospective students should possess the aptitude, passion and skills required for commercial, economic, numeracy and mathematical disciplines. The ideal candidate should have: Good listening and interpersonal skills Logical reasoning, persuasiveness and sound judgement Honesty and integrity Ability to digest and convert large volumes of information and data The emotional intelligence needed to view a challenge objectively and address it proactively Attention to detail Ability to prioritise tasks and manage time effectively
fields.career_opportunities: First step towards becoming attorneys, legal advisors, advocates, prosecutors, presiding officers (magistrates or judges) and academics in the legal field.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Marketing Management
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Marketing Management degree provides a theoretical foundation of marketing principles complemented by practical projects over three years of study. The programme equips students with the ability to develop, plan and implement integrated marketing communication strategies. Students are trained to communicate a marketing vision and marketing ideas for products and services in various industries. Industry partners provide real challenges for which students find solutions, which are presented to the industry partner. These challenges may include: Developing integrated marketing communication campaigns Designing sales or in-store promotions Creating social media marketing campaigns Conducting market and consumer analyses The ideal candidates for this programme are: People-oriented Able to think out of the box Love brands Engaged in technology Versatile Go-getters Optimistic Companies that employ our graduates: Small to large businesses Advertising agencies Fast-moving consumer goods companies (eg Unilever, Coca-Cola) Public relations agencies Service companies in the finance, motor, travel and insurance industries (eg Bridgestone, Discovery) Marketing research companies For more information visit https://www.up.ac.za/marketing-management
fields.career_opportunities: Product management, customer service management, customer relationship management, strategic marketing, sales management, brand management, advertising management, media planning, marketing research management and promotions management.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Investment Management
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Investment Management programme focuses on the global investment environment and includes topics covering all facets of investment management. The degree prepares you for career opportunities in the dynamic world of investments by offering access to money markets, fixed-income markets, equity markets, derivatives markets and portfolio management. The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Investment Management degree at the University of Pretoria is an Affiliated Program with CFA InstituteÂ®. Academic institutions that embed a significant portion of the CFA Program Candidate Body of Knowledge (CBOK)â including the Code of Ethics and Standards of Professional Conduct. An affiliated university signals to its students and to employers that the curriculum is closely aligned with the practice of investment management and is helpful to students preparing for the CFAÂ®  Program exams. CFA Institute and CFA are registered trademarks owned by CFA Institute. The ideal candidate should have: Strong mathematical skills Diligence Analytical skills The ability to think independently Problem-solving skills An inquiring mind Integrity Attention to detail For more information visit www.up.ac.za/financial-management .
fields.career_opportunities: Portfolio/fund manager, investment analyst, risk manager/analyst, quantitative analyst, financial advisor/planner, wealth manager, investment strategist.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Financial Management Sciences
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Financial Management Sciences degree combines the disciplines of financial management, internal auditing and taxation in a single degree with the aim of giving students more options for further study. This introduction to multiple fields empowers students to select their area of specialisation at the honours level. Students benefit from recognition by leading professional bodies and the opportunity to pursue postgraduate qualifications. The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Financial Management Sciences degree is accredited by: Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) South African Institute of Chartered Accountants (SAICA) for the AGA(SA) qualification These endorsements confirm that graduates receive world-class education, paving the way for graduates to pursue various advanced professional qualifications. The University of Pretoria is one of only 12 universities in the world accredited as a Center for Internal Audit Excellenceâthe highest level of accreditation awarded by the International Institute of Internal Auditors. To excel in this programme, students should be: Passionate about numbers Attentive to details Innovative in solving problems Analytical Able to communicate well Good at interpersonal engagements For more information visit www.up.ac.za/financial-management .
fields.career_opportunities: Careers with a focus on:Business accounting: Financial reporting specialistsFinancial management: Management accountants, management consultants, financial advisors, cost accountants and financial managersTaxation: Tax advisors, tax auditors in public practice, tax policy designers, tax professionals, tax compliance managers, SARS auditors and tax risk managersInternal auditing: Internal auditors, risk officers, forensic auditors, IT auditors, performance auditors, environmental auditors, compliance officers, government auditors  and  governance auditors

fields.name: BCom 3-year programme
fields.description: The three-year Bachelor of Commerce programme is aimed at training students in the Economic and Management Sciences, and provides them with a broad understanding of the various functions in the areas of economics, finance and management. It allows students to select modules that align with their career interests and the sectors they wish to pursue. This programme is suitable for students with broad academic interests that are not met by the fixed curricula of other Bachelor of Commerce degrees. Students are offered the opportunity to determine their own career outcomes, within limits, as it enables an in-depth exploration of various elective modules during undergraduate studies This programme caters to students who: Are interested in obtaining a Bachelor of Commerce degree Are competent and interested in diverse study areas Are undecided about which major to select Require flexibility in selecting modules Do not want to specialise too early in their studies Depending on curriculum composition, students can choose to specialise in a specific field by pursuing postgraduate studies in: Economic sciences Financial sciences Management sciences
fields.career_opportunities: Although the degree does not lead to a specific vocational outcome, it does, to some extent, offer students opportunities to determine their own career paths as it allows them to compile their own curricula with a view to future work opportunities in all sectors.

fields.name: BAdmin specialising in Public Administration and International Relations
fields.description: The Bachelor of Administration specialising in Public Administration and International Relations programme prepares students for a career in the public sector. The degree provides in-depth knowledge of certain administrative and management practices in the South African and international public sectors. It focuses on the three spheres of government, namely the local, provincial and national levels. Key areas covered in the programme include: Public financial and human resources management International administration and management Policy and organisation studies Ethics Project management Intergovernmental relations Administrative justice The Bachelor of Administration degree is the only programme in the Faculty of EMS that combines public sector management with specialisations such as politics and international relations. The programme exposes students to the management environment in both the public and private sectors. It enhances studentsâ understanding of what it means to be good and responsible citizens. The ideal candidates should: Have the desire, commitment and dedication to serve their community, their city and/or their country Believe in and want to be part of the moral and legal obligation that the state has towards creating a better life for all Our graduates are employed by: Government departments, embassies, consulates, High Commissions, parastatals, non-governmental organisations, private sector companies that work extensively with government contracts. For more information visit www.up.ac.za/BAdmin .
fields.career_opportunities: Diplomats, political analysts, managers in the public sector, local government and the private sector, non-governmental organisations, international organisations and embassies. Graduates are often employed in human resourcse sections and planning and general administrative divisions.

fields.name: BCom 4-year programme
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce [4-year programme] is designed for students who want to study commerce but do not yet meet the entrance requirements for the standard threeâyear BCom. This programme includes a foundation year that helps students build the academic skills they need to succeed. It provides extra support and a stronger base, giving students the opportunity to follow an extended curriculum and still pursue a career in commerce.
fields.career_opportunities: are dependent on the Bachelor of Commerce degree that a student continues with.

fields.name: BCom in Accounting Sciences
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting Sciences is a three-year programme, followed by the one-year Postgraduate Diploma in Accounting Sciences (PGDAS). Together, these programmes meet the education requirements for the Chartered Accountant qualification, CA(SA). Successful completion of these two study programmes will provide access to the Initial Assessment of Competence (IAC), which is the first of two professional exams set by the South African Institute of Chartered Accounting (SAICA), Thereafter, a three-year traineeship needs to be completed and the applicable professional qualifying examinations written during this time for eligibility to register as a CA(SA). The Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting Sciences degree and the Postgraduate Diploma in Accounting Sciences are endorsed by SAICA and form part of the requirements for training as a CA(SA), which is the highest professional designation in accounting in South Africa, with international recognition. The undergraduate degree is also endorsed by the Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA). Over and above the prestigious CA(SA) qualification, the 3-year Bachelor of Commerce in Accounting Sciences degree also provides access to the following Bachelor of Commerce Honours degrees (subject to selection): BComHons (Financial Management) BComHons (Internal Auditing) BComHons (Taxation) The characteristics of students who excel in this programme include: Ethical Critical thinker Detail oriented Interested in business Self-motivated Qualified CAs(SA) are able to fulfil business and finance roles in a variety of industries. They tend to specialise in financial reporting, auditing and assurance, tax, financial management, information technology, management accounting, forensic accounting and insolvency. There is also the possibility of working in academia, the public or private sector and nongovernmental organisations. Those who have a CA(SA) qualification also succeed in CEO, CFO, vice-president, chairp
fields.career_opportunities: First step towards qualifying as chartered accountants, external auditors, taxation professionals and advisors, financial directors, managers or entrepreneurs.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Information Systems
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Information Systems degree equips students with expertise in designing, building, implementing, and securing information systems that serve society at large. The programme enables graduates to combine technical knowledge with personal and professional interests, preparing them to create and apply computer-based solutions in real-world organisational contexts. This degree focuses on understanding how people interact with information and technology, and on developing systems that enhance productivity, collaboration, and decision-making. By bridging business and technology, students gain the skills to design solutions that make everyday work more efficient, effective, and secure. The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Information Systems programme at UP is the only degree in South Africa that is internationally accredited by the Computing Accreditation Commission of the Accreditation Board for Engineering and Technology (ABET) of the USA. Developments in IT enhance the business environment by enabling companies to do more in less time and provide management information faster and more accurately.  IT is the gateway to the international business world and makes it possible to complete business transactions within seconds. It is jointly presented with the Faculty of Engineering, Built Environment and Information Technology. Our graduates are employed in all commercial sectors, including: Finance Insurance Telecommunication Mining Software development Consulting organisations Education Retail Characteristics of students who excel in this programme are: Problem solvers with analytical minds Creative thinkers Good communicators Good at working in a team Good at working with people Able to deal with ambiguity For more information visit www.up.ac.za/informatics
fields.career_opportunities: Managers in the information technology environment, computer software or hardware support services, computer systems analysts, financial information systems analysts, business analysts, information facilitators, IT training officers, systems developers, business systems analysts.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Agribusiness Management
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Agribusiness Management programme provides an overview of the global agricultural and food sector. It highlights how business and science issues affect how the agricultural sector functions, giving students a balanced perspective on this important field. The programme cultivates problem solvers who are prepared to contribute to ensuring food security for the world's population. The programme caters for students with diverse interests and proficiencies, ranging from business to science. It is jointly presented with the UP Faculty of Natural and Agricultural Sciences. This programme is the ideal choice for students who: Have strong business acumen Love the outdoors Enjoy science For more information visit www.up.ac.za/agricultural-economics-extension-and-rural-development .
fields.career_opportunities: The degree offers diverse career choices. Opportunities exist in banking, finance and insurance; policy design and implementation; research and consultation; food processing and manufacturing; commodity trading on global and local stock markets; logistics and supply chain operations; and business management in agricultural companies, to name but a few.

fields.name: BCom specialising in Statistics and Data Science
fields.description: The Bachelor of Commerce specialising in Statistics and Data Science is a three-year full-time programme that trains students in the science of extracting information from data. This qualification equips graduates for employment in data science environments, expanding career opportunities across any industry. It provides the foundation for scientifically accountable conclusions, planning, and estimation. This programme also enhances studentsâ understating of statistics which is a vital part of modern life and business and is used daily to make sense of the world around us. For example, it helps them to understand how social media platforms personalise content and suggestions for different people. This programme is presented jointly with UP Faculty of Natural and Agricultural Sciences. Characteristics of students who excel in this programme are: Strong numerical skills Interest in computers and programming Logical reasoning ability Interest in planning and in analysing problems Good language and communication skills Strong problem-solving skills Inquisitive Innovative Team player Critical thinking skills Agile mind Adaptability Accreditations/Certifications: Students in this programme can obtain Statistical Analysis Software (SAS) certification in areas such as: Statistical learning Big data analytics For more information visit www.up.ac.za/statistics
fields.career_opportunities: Data scientists, statistical analysts in several industries, researchers, consultants and lecturers.

fields.name: Bachelor of Arts [BA] 3-year programme
fields.description: In the Faculty of Humanities, more students choose a Bachelor of Arts degree than any other programme. Its adaptability enables students to explore disciplines and proceed with those best suited to their interests and abilities. At the first-year level, Academic information management (AIM) and Academic literacy (ALL) are fundamental (compulsory) modules. In compiling their programmes, students have an extraordinarily wide choice. Since the programme allows for two core disciplines, students have more than one option for postgraduate study. They therfore have an alternative, should they not comply with the selection requirements for postgraduate study in one of the disciplines. With good planning, students who complete the Bachelor of Arts programme have the same chance of being selected for postgraduate studies in a particular discipline as those who enrol for the more specialised programmes.
fields.career_opportunities: Depending on the choice of disciplines majoring in the Bachelor of Arts lays the foundation for further studies and work in the following fields:Community engagement and developmentCounsellingCultural studiesDiplomacy and politicsEducationGovernanceHeritage conservationJournalismLanguage servicesMedia studiesPolicy analysisResearchSocietal analysisTranslationWork and organisational studiesNote: Students who wish to become psychologists must complete a Bachelor of Arts majoring in Psychology, the Bachelor of Social Science Honours specialising in Psychology and the Master of Arts in Clinical, Counselling or Research Psychology. Careers: Clinical, counselling and research psychologists (various research career options). Contact the Board of Psychology at the Health Professions Council of South Africa at click here for information on the requirements.

fields.name: BA specialising in Languages
fields.description: Bachelor of Arts specialising in Languages equips students with integrated communication skills and knowledge of the grammar, literature and culture of at least two languages of their choice. Students can major in Afrikaans, English, German, French, Portuguese, Spanish, isiZulu, Ndebele, Sepedi, Greek, Hebrew or Latin, and may add more languages and/or other modules from other disciplines in the Faculty to these language disciplines.
fields.career_opportunities: Advertising, media and communication, copywriting and creative writing, text and document design, editing, language planning, language teaching and training, lexicography (compilation of dictionaries), technical writing, public relations, translation, publishing, diplomatic relations or tourism.
